commit.message:
feat(ux): polish pairing completion flows and settings diagnostics surfaces

Delivers the remaining Homey UX-polish slices by improving post-add guidance for node onboarding and surfacing runtime health directly in app settings.

- node pairing flow now includes a custom import summary step: list_devices -> add_devices -> import_summary
- node driver pair session now exposes import_summary:get_status with live discovery/import/pending metrics and warning diagnostics
- added node import summary custom view UI with refreshable connection/import status
- upgraded settings page UX with runtime diagnostics panel (ZWJS connection, imported nodes, recommendation/backfill counts, artifact state, operator warnings)
- preserved template-safe pairing model while adding richer post-add UX via custom views
- updated pairing-flow/driver harness tests and synced architecture/ADR/roadmap/sprint docs

Tests: npm --prefix co.lazylabs.zwavejs2homey run test

feat(bridge-tools): enrich read-only diagnostics with node identity, profile, and mapping context

Expands bridge diagnostics snapshots and Bridge Tools UI so operators can triage nodes from bridge view without opening each node device.

commit.message:
docs(references): reorganize EtherCAT spec chapter docs

Summary:
- Add a dedicated `docs/references/ethercat-spec/` folder for spec
  chapter summaries derived from the three EtherCAT PDFs.
- Rewrite chapter docs into technical tone, remove narrative
  header/footer text, and standardize chapter ordering with `01-21`
  filename prefixes.
- Add `01-llm-reference-index.md` as the LLM-first entrypoint and
  update AGENTS/README references to the new path.

Rationale:
- Keeps spec-derived guidance separate from implementation references
  and makes chapter loading deterministic.
- Gives the LLM a stable index file to decide which chapter files to
  load into context for a given task.

Validation:
- Verified ordered file set (`01-21`) exists in `ethercat-spec/`.
- Verified index links and AGENTS/README paths point to numbered files.
- Did not run code tests (documentation-only changes).

commit.message:
fix(mep): stabilize controller-lock + stop/resume + entry dispatch (IssueOps #2400 shirnugui) (#2902)

* chore: open PR shell for Issue #2400 shirnugui (Codex will fill)

* fix(mep): stabilize controller-lock + stop/resume + entry dispatch (IssueOps #2400 shirnugui)

### Motivation
- Prevent recursive writeback PR spawning and related failure modes by making controller-lock, entry dispatch, and stop/resume behaviour explicit and single-source-of-truth.
- Eliminate guesswork around controller identity by fixing a strict controller-label SSOT and forbidding label inference.
- Keep `entry_clean` as a stable entrypoint even when downstream dispatch/flows are disabled so the system can record outcomes and not fail noisily.
- Make diagnostics and runner I/O robust (check-runs fallback and UTF-8 decode strategy) so jobs/log-less failure modes are diagnosable and Windows cp932 decode errors do not crash the runner.

### Description
- Enforced controller-lock validation and optional exact-match in writeback creation by updating `tools/mep_writeback_create_pr.ps1` to `STOP_HARD` if the controller label is missing/invalid or mismatched and added regex `^mep:controller=CHAT_[A-Za-z0-9_]+$` as SSOT.
- Canonicalized and hardened entry workflows: made downstream dispatch optional and non-fatal with `continue-on-error` and added an `Entry summary` step; updated workflows: ` .github/workflows/mep_entry_clean.yml`, `mep_entry_clean_dispatch.yml`, and `mep_writeback_bundle_dispatch_entry.yml` to accept controller inputs instead of relying on push triggers.
- Simplified OP-1 evidence dispatch entry to require a `controller_label` input and validate it before triggering downstream (`.github/workflows/mep_evidence_writeback_dispatch_entry.yml`).
- Added operational helper scripts: `tools/mep_loop_switch.sh` (single `freeze|resume|status` switch for loop/writeback workflows) and `tools/mep_checkruns_fallback.sh` (enumerate commit `check-runs` and `details_url` for diagnostics).
- Hardened runner subprocess decoding to use `encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"` in `tools/runner/runner.py` to avoid Windows cp932 UnicodeDecodeError crashes.
- Added documentation `docs/MEP/ISSUE2400_SHIRNUGUI_FIXATE.md` describing the controller-lock SSOT, freeze/resume procedure, manual entry guidance, check-runs fallback, and restart/recovery commands (including the exact controller label for this operation: `mep:controller=CHAT_CHAT_20260305T140500Z_9A1F`).

### Testing
- Loaded/validated modified workflows with `python`/`yaml.safe_load` for `mep_entry_clean.yml`, `mep_entry_clean_dispatch.yml`, `mep_writeback_bundle_dispatch_entry.yml`, and `mep_evidence_writeback_dispatch_entry.yml`, and each YAML parse succeeded.
- Compiled `tools/runner/runner.py` with `python -m py_compile` and the file passed bytecode compilation.
- Performed shell syntax checks on new helper scripts (`bash -n tools/mep_loop_switch.sh tools/mep_checkruns_fallback.sh`) and they passed.
- Environment-limited operations: pushing the branch and interacting with GitHub via `gh` could not be completed in this environment (`origin` remote not configured and `gh` CLI not available), so PR creation/merge and runtime end-to-end verification on GitHub could not be executed here.

* ci: make required checks autofix/build-artifacts report success on PR

---------

Co-authored-by: mep-loop-bot <mep-loop-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mep): add fatal guard + gh api error logging in poller (#2900)

* fix(mep): add fatal guard + gh api error logging in poller

### Motivation
- Harden the merge-finish poller so `gh api` failures surface useful diagnostics and the job does not crash the workflow on unexpected exceptions while preserving best-effort behavior.

### Description
- Update `.github/workflows/mep_merge_finish_poller.yml` Python heredoc to `import traceback` and strengthen `sh(cmd)` to call `subprocess.check_output(cmd, text=True, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)` and catch `subprocess.CalledProcessError` to print a `[poll][sh_error]` log with the command, return code, and command output before re-raising.
- Wrap the main polling loop in a `try/except/finally` so unexpected exceptions emit a `[poll][fatal]` line and full traceback while keeping best-effort continuation semantics and ensuring the final `print` of `scanned`/`posted` always runs.
- Preserve existing API path normalization, comment-dedupe guard, and best-effort comment posting logic, and keep the `run: |` block indentation (including the `q=` line) intact to avoid YAML parsing issues.

### Testing
- Validated the edited workflow file is valid YAML by running `python3` with `yaml.safe_load` on `.github/workflows/mep_merge_finish_poller.yml`, which succeeded.
- Confirmed the modified file diff contains only the intended Python heredoc changes and that the workflow block indentation remained unchanged using local file inspection commands.

* fix(mep): add fatal guard + gh api error logging in poller

* fix(mep): add fatal guard + gh api error logging in poller

* fix(mep): add fatal guard + gh api error logging in poller

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(workflow): refresh orchestration template defaults

Summary:
- Set the Linear project slug in WORKFLOW.md and expand repository setup
  hooks to include IgH/SOEM references.
- Update sandbox defaults to danger-full-access in the template and add
  a before_run fast-forward merge step.
- Add explicit GitHub connectivity blocker handling and route Human
  Review transitions through publish success or confirmed blocker.

Rationale:
- The workflow template should reflect the current unattended execution
  environment and expected publish failure handling.
- Capturing blocker protocol in one place reduces retry churn and keeps
  ticket state transitions consistent.

Validation:
- Not run (workflow template/documentation changes).

docs(agent): prefer breaking API changes pre-release

Summary:
- Add a hard rule in AGENTS.md that this pre-release library should
  prefer API clarity over backward compatibility.
- Mirror the same rule in WORKFLOW.md implementation guidance so both
  execution paths apply the same policy.

Rationale:
- The project is not released yet, so compatibility shims and dual-path
  behavior add noise without user value.
- Making this explicit reduces conservative edits and keeps the API
  direction consistent.

Validation:
- Not run (docs-only changes).

parser: fix markdown headings in .autodoc (#312)

Fix plain-text mode so markdown-style headings are handled before
treating the line as plain text. This restores heading aliases in
.autodoc files.

commit.message:
homey: harden node pairing against slow zwjs lookups

Prevents Add Node pairing spinner lock by adding defensive timeouts to node-list, zone lookup, and per-node state/icon inference, plus bounded icon-inference concurrency.

On timeout/failure, pairing now degrades gracefully and still returns candidates instead of blocking indefinitely.

Adds harness regression coverage for hanging node-state lookups and syncs sprint notes.

- 为任务日志查询新增 session_id 过滤并支持与 run_count 组合过滤\n- 补齐 repository/service/API 白盒与回归用例，覆盖空白 session_id 与空结果语义\n- 更新 CHANGELOG Unreleased 记录本次能力与测试边界，未修改版本号

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Move Elixir observability dashboard to Phoenix (#29)

#### Context

Replace the custom Elixir observability TCP server with Phoenix while
keeping the shipped escript and existing operational API calls working
the same way they did before.

#### TL;DR

*Move the Elixir observability dashboard and API onto Phoenix without
breaking the escript or curl workflows.*

#### Summary

- replace the custom HTTP server with a Phoenix endpoint, router,
controller, and shared presenter
- add a LiveView operations dashboard with PubSub-driven updates and
embed its CSS and JS assets in code
- preserve runtime compatibility by accepting form-style POSTs and
extending the endpoint coverage around those paths

#### Alternatives

- keep extending the hand-rolled TCP server, but that keeps custom
parsing and routing complexity in the app
- serve dashboard assets from priv directories, but the shipped escript
cannot rely on those files existing on disk

#### Test Plan

- [x] `make -C elixir all`
- [x] `mix -C elixir test test/symphony_elixir/extensions_test.exs`
- [x] `./bin/symphony
--i-understand-that-this-will-be-running-without-the-usual-guardrails
--port 40123`
- [x] `curl -si http://127.0.0.1:40123/dashboard.css`
- [x] `curl -si http://127.0.0.1:40123/vendor/phoenix/phoenix.js`
- [x] `curl -si -X POST -d '' http://127.0.0.1:40123/api/v1/refresh`
- [x] `curl -si -X POST -d '' http://127.0.0.1:40123/api/v1/state`

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

application to avoid stale disabled decode state.

commit.message:
[fix](trx-server): hard-gate CW decode to CW/CWR modes

Enforce CW decode processing only when mode is CW/CWR and decoder
is enabled, even within the receive loop, and remove CW path RMS
zero-gating to preserve weak tone decoding.

commit.message:
[fix](trx-cw): stabilize auto tone scan window

Keep manual CW tone setting range at 100-10000 Hz, but limit auto-tone
scanning to 300-1200 Hz to avoid locking onto high-frequency noise.
Retain Nyquist-safe upper clamping.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-cw): widen tone scan range and clamp by Nyquist

Expand CW decoder tone scan/control range to 100-10000 Hz and cap the
upper bound by sample-rate Nyquist to keep detection stable.

[fix](trx-frontend): fix CW picker visibility and widen tone range

Fix CW picker redraw when the decoder sub-tab becomes visible to avoid
white/blank canvas rendering.
Widen CW tone picker/input range to 100-10000 Hz and raise CW/CWR
bandwidth max to 9 kHz.

commit.message:
[fix](trx-server): gate near-silent audio before decode

Apply an RMS gate to near-silent decoder input frames and zero them
before APRS/CW/FT8/WSPR processing to avoid false decodes from
unsquelched/no-signal noise.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

commit.message:
(docs) Migrate website from DocFX to MkDocs Material

Replace the DocFX documentation build with MkDocs Material and migrate
docs tooling to uv with locked dependencies.

Keep Perlang example rendering by translating both code-perlang tags and
fenced perlang blocks through a MkDocs hook, while preserving custom
Perlang syntax highlighting and asciinema embeds.

Remove obsolete DocFX files/templates and update GitHub/GitLab CI to
validate website builds with the new pipeline.

Closes https://gitlab.perlang.org/perlang/perlang/-/issues/515

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

DocFX-based website build, so docs build breakage is caught in MRs and
on the default branch.

Semi-related to https://gitlab.perlang.org/perlang/perlang/-/issues/515;
will be updated to run MkDocs-based website generation once the
conversion is complete.
